mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds +0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rate-increasing months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% annualized during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, prompting central bank tightening expectations that suppress S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive 20d returns in >58% of late-cycle instances, or predictive power vanishes when applied to early-cycle or recession regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth revisions above 3.0% confirm robust economic momentum that reinforces equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of high-GDP quarters, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat or declining federal funds rate creates a negative real yield environment that erodes fixed-income alternatives, forcing capital into equities and lifting S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Negative real yield periods produce negative 60-day equity returns in >55% of cases, or the joint condition fails to outperform single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 250,000 during post-recession recovery phases confirms labor market normalization that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: High payroll growth months generate negative 20d returns in >60% of recovery-phase instances, or the signal loses predictive power outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals abrupt supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate output and triggers immediate equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with statistical significance.
